Recurrent Neural Network (RNN)
Long sequences are difficult to model with standard RNNs
Vanishing gradients make long-range dependencies hard to learn
Exploding gradients can often be controlled with gradient clipping
RNN: Computational Graph
An unrolled recurrent neural network
The same weight and bias shared across all the steps

7
Recurrent Neural Networks
The same function and the same set of parameters are reused at every time step.
RNN Parameter Sharing Across Time

possible_slide_title: Why Standard RNNs Struggle with Long-Term Dependencies
visible_text:
Why Standard RNNs Struggle with Long-Term Dependencies
Short-Term Dependencies
Relevant information is nearby.
Standard RNNs can often learn it
Example: Predict the last word in
“the clouds are in the
sky
,”
8
Long-Term Dependencies
Relevant information is far away
Gradients may vanish over many time steps
Learning becomes difficult
Example: Understanding a word or event much earlier in a sentence or document
Standard RNNs often struggle when useful information must travel across many time steps. Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) was designed to address this problem.
Relevant information is far away

Activation Functions in LSTM: Sigmoid and Tanh
Sigmoid
Tanh
Output range: (0,1)
Often used for gates because it acts like a soft on/off control
Output range: (-1,1)
Often used for candidate memory and hidden state values
Sigmoid controls how much information passes through; tanh creates bounded content values for the cell and hidden state.

Multi-Layer LSTM
One LSTM layer processes the original input sequence
The next LSTM layer processes the hidden outputs from the previous layer
This creates a deeper sequence model
hidden size = number of units in one LSTM layer
num_layers
= number of stacked LSTM layers
nn.LSTM
(
input_size
=10,
hidden_size
=32,
num_layers
=2,
batch_first
=True)
The output sequence from one LSTM layer becomes the input sequence to the next layer.

LSTM Summary
RNNs process data one time step at a time.
Standard RNNs struggle with long-term dependencies.
LSTM uses cell
state + gates
to manage memory.
nn.LSTM
supports flexible sequence modeling in
PyTorch
.
Common patterns: one-to-many, many-to-one, many-to-many, seq-to-seq, bidirectional.
47
LSTM improves sequence learning by preserving useful information over time.
